title: Splash, Dash!
content:
"Splash, dash!
        Rumble and crash!
        Here come the beavers gay;
        See what they do,
        Rosy, for you,
        Because you helped me one day."


        "They come at my call;
        And though they are small,
        They'll dig the passage clear:
        I never forget;
        We'll save them yet,
        For love of Rosy dear."
meaning:
This evocative piece by Louisa May Alcott, titled "Splash, Dash!", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
